speaker: Generic Blocker / Street NPCs
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for reusable generic blockers, street NPCs, crowd witnesses, queue NPCs, and ambient city characters in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Reusable route blockers and soft gates
Street flavour NPCs for Office Street, Old Town, Residential District, Business District, Airport Road, Riverside Towers, City Hall, Protest Square, and court-adjacent areas
Crowd witnesses who make the Legal Quarter feel alive without replacing named characters
Hint carriers for chapter objectives, evidence routes, mini-games, and district transitions
Ambient civilians who show human consequences of legal and bureaucratic decisions
Chapter 5 protest and notice-recipient crowd lines
Standardised multi-variant file using variant_id for mechanical filtering

voice_profile.core_voice: small, grounded, readable NPC voices: practical, funny, worried, nosy, tired, locally specific, and short enough for RPG dialogue balloons
comedy_engine: ordinary people interpret absurd legal events through everyday city life
route blockers know more about the plot than they admit
bureaucratic chaos leaks into street gossip
crowds misread sophisticated litigation as very visible practical trouble
minor NPCs make high legal stakes concrete through queues, doors, notices, scooters, windows, coffee, and paper
do: keep lines short and mechanically useful
make blocker lines clearly explain why a route is closed or what unlocks it
use variant_id to allow scripts to filter by NPC type
make street NPCs hint without solving the case
make them funny but not random
do_not: make every generic NPC sound identical
make route blockers feel like invisible walls only
confuse ordinary office Printer with Industrial Printer
make crowd lines too long for RPG text boxes

variant_ids.route_blocker: Explicit route-gate NPCs who block or unlock map areas.
variant_ids.office_street_pedestrian: Office Street pedestrians reacting to Pig & Swine and early office chaos.
variant_ids.legal_intern: Young legal interns and junior clerks who misunderstand the situation usefully.
variant_ids.courier: Courier and delivery NPCs tied to filings, notices, envelopes, and deadlines.
variant_ids.queue_citizen: People waiting in court, City Hall, cafés, or counters.
variant_ids.tenant_neighbour: Residential District neighbours and tenants in Chapter 2.
variant_ids.building_worker: Maintenance, cleaner, lift, and building-service NPCs.
variant_ids.business_district_worker: Business District workers, print-shop customers, rival-firm assistants.
variant_ids.traffic_npc: Road, scooter, cyclist, and pedestrian NPCs for traversal and mini-games.
variant_ids.airport_passenger: Airport-area civilians around Chapter 4 travel evidence.
variant_ids.city_hall_civilian: City Hall visitors and notice recipients.
variant_ids.protest_square_civilian: Chapter 5 protesters, volunteers, and affected residents.
variant_ids.press_npc: Journalists and camera crews around public controversy.
variant_ids.postgame_civilian: Free-roam epilogue NPCs reacting to outcomes.

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft for later rewriting into godot/data/dialogues/dialogues.json. Use variant_id to filter subtypes mechanically.
